Hero.desc: Building responsive, interactive web applications. 6 years across startups and scale-ups — open to new opportunities.

about.title: About me
paragraphs: I'm Marta, a frontend developer based in Prague.

I build web applications with React, Next.js and TypeScript. I have 6 years of experience working on product and client-facing projects, from public websites and e-commerce flows to larger frontend applications and modernization projects.

I enjoy creating clean, maintainable interfaces and working on features where UI, data handling and application state come together. Recently, I’ve been focusing more on product frontend, GraphQL, state management and improving existing frontend architecture.
I’m also interested in gradually moving towards fullstack development, especially with Node.js.

email.title: Let's Connect
email.message: I'm currently looking for new opportunities, my inbox is always open. Whether you have a question or just want to say hi, I'll try my best to get back to you!

0.title: Prospector
0.description: BizMachine sales intelligence platform for company data search and segmentation. I contributed to the frontend rewrite to Next.js 14, search and filtering experience, GraphQL integration, Zustand state management and shared UI components.
highlights: BizMachine sales intelligence platform for company data search and segmentation.
Frontend contribution to the rewrite to Next.js 14.
Search and filtering UX, GraphQL integration, Zustand and shared UI components.

caseStudy.subtitle: Sales Intelligence Platform
overview: Prospector is a BizMachine sales intelligence product for company search, market segmentation, exports and CRM workflows.
I contributed to the frontend rewrite and modernization of a data-heavy product where search, filters and segmentation are core user workflows.
caseStudy.myRole: I worked as a frontend developer for BizMachine. My contribution focused on Next.js 14 screens, search and filtering UX, GraphQL integration, Zustand state management and shared UI components.

workSections.title: Modern Frontend Architecture
paragraphs: Built and refactored product screens during the migration to Next.js 14, helping move the app towards a cleaner, more maintainable frontend structure.

workSections.title: Complex Data Workflows
paragraphs: Worked on search results, filters, company segments and data-driven interactions where users need to quickly narrow down large company datasets.

workSections.title: Typed Data and State
paragraphs: Integrated GraphQL queries, generated types and hooks, and used Zustand for UI state around filters, selected data and shared interactions.

caseStudy.whyItMattered: The frontend had to make complex company data fast, searchable and understandable for sales users.
caseStudy.projectShows: Modern Next.js product development, GraphQL data handling, state management and maintainable UI for complex data workflows.

1.title: Case Management Platform
1.description: Enterprise case-management platform for dynamic business process forms. I worked on the React/Redux frontend layer, configurable widgets, validation logic, case overview and customer-specific UI customization.
highlights: Enterprise case-management platform for dynamic business process forms.
React/Redux frontend layer with configurable widgets and validation logic.
Case overview, process UI and customer-specific customization.

caseStudy.subtitle: Enterprise Case Management Platform
overview: Enterprise workflow and case-management platform for dynamic business processes, cases, tasks and process-driven forms.
My work focused on configurable frontend UI, form behavior and customer-specific customization without exposing confidential product details.
caseStudy.myRole: I worked as a React developer on the React/Redux frontend layer, mostly around dynamic form widgets, validation logic, process navigation and localized customer-specific UI.

workSections.title: Dynamic Forms and Widgets
paragraphs: Built and adjusted configurable widgets used inside process-driven forms, including search fields, document fields, date ranges, Calendly integration and workflow-specific inputs.

workSections.title: Validation and Case UI
paragraphs: Worked on required states, validation errors, read-only/preview modes, case overview screens and process navigation.

workSections.title: Integration and Customization
paragraphs: Connected UI to REST APIs, worked with i18next localization and implemented customer-specific styling and behavior for different deployments.

caseStudy.whyItMattered: The UI had to stay consistent while supporting many process configurations, widget types and customer-specific workflows.
caseStudy.projectShows: Enterprise React/Redux development, dynamic form rendering, reusable widgets, validation, localization and configurable UI.

2.title: Sherwood Digital
2.description: Frontend work across e-commerce projects and internal platforms. I worked with React, Redux, REST APIs, checkout flows, forms, localization, reusable components and responsive templates.
highlights: Frontend work across e-commerce projects and internal platforms.
React, Redux and REST API integration.
Checkout flows, forms, localization, reusable components and responsive templates.

caseStudy.subtitle: E-commerce & Internal Platforms
overview: Sherwood Digital gave me broad frontend experience across client e-commerce projects, internal platforms and reusable React components.
The work was varied: production templates, checkout flows, forms, API-connected modules and responsive UI.
caseStudy.myRole: I worked as a web / frontend developer across client-facing e-commerce work and internal tools, using React, Redux, REST APIs, forms, localization and LESS/SCSS.

workSections.title: E-commerce UI and Checkout
paragraphs: Worked on responsive e-commerce templates and a checkout flow with cart state, addresses, shipping/payment selection and order summary.

workSections.title: Forms, State and APIs
paragraphs: Used Redux, Redux Thunk, REST APIs, Axios, react-hook-form and Yup for multi-step flows and data-driven UI.

workSections.title: Reusable Components
paragraphs: Built shared React components and worked on internal platform modules, localization and responsive production styling.

caseStudy.whyItMattered: The value was variety: real client projects, transactional flows, internal tools and shared components.
caseStudy.projectShows: Adaptability across codebases, production UI delivery, forms, state management, REST API integration and reusable React components.

3.title: Socialbakers Website
3.description: Public-facing company website for Socialbakers, a social media marketing and analytics platform. I worked on website maintenance, new pages and components, responsive UI updates, production bug fixes and rewriting older PHP-based parts of the website to Next.js.
highlights: Public-facing company website for Socialbakers, a social media marketing and analytics platform.
Website maintenance, new pages and components.
Responsive UI updates, production bug fixes and rewriting older PHP parts to Next.js.

caseStudy.subtitle: Public Company Website
overview: Public company website for Socialbakers, including marketing pages, careers content and a WordPress blog.
I worked on frontend maintenance, new pages, responsive UI and the gradual move from older PHP-based parts to Next.js.
caseStudy.myRole: I worked as a web developer on new pages and components, production bug fixes, responsive layout updates and PHP-to-Next.js rewrite work.

workSections.title: Public Website Delivery
paragraphs: Maintained and improved live public pages, including layout updates, content-related changes and responsive fixes.

workSections.title: Components and Bug Fixing
paragraphs: Built reusable frontend components and fixed UI issues across a production website used by real visitors.

workSections.title: Legacy to Next.js
paragraphs: Supported the rewrite of older PHP-based website parts into a more modern Next.js frontend.

caseStudy.projectShows: Public-facing website maintenance, responsive UI, reusable components, production bug fixing and gradual frontend modernization.

4.title: Socialbakers Careers
4.description: Careers website for Socialbakers. I worked on frontend implementation, responsive UI, job listings, filtering and Greenhouse integration.
highlights: Job listings, filtering and Greenhouse integration.

caseStudy.subtitle: Careers Website
overview: Public careers website for Socialbakers with hiring content, job listings, filtering and Greenhouse integration.
caseStudy.myRole: I worked on frontend implementation, responsive layout updates, job listing components, filtering and content updates.

workSections.title: Careers UI
paragraphs: Implemented and maintained responsive careers pages aligned with the Socialbakers visual style.

workSections.title: Jobs and Greenhouse
paragraphs: Worked on job listing views, filtering by location or department and frontend display of Greenhouse job data.

workSections.title: Production Updates
paragraphs: Supported content changes, UI adjustments and production fixes for a public company website.

caseStudy.projectShows: Responsive public websites, job listing interfaces, filtering, external service integration and production maintenance.

5.title: Notes App
5.description: Personal fullstack notes app with authentication, nested pages and block-based editing.

caseStudy.subtitle: Fullstack Notes Application
caseStudy.problem: I wanted to explore a more product-like notes experience with authentication, persistent data, nested pages and flexible content editing.
caseStudy.myRole: I designed and built the app end to end, focusing on editor interactions, page structure, authentication and Supabase-backed persistence.
whatIDid: Built authentication and database-backed notes with Supabase
Implemented block-based editing for different content types
Added nested pages and page hierarchy
Created drag-and-drop reordering interactions
Composed the editor and navigation from reusable React components
caseStudy.outcome: The project helped me practice product thinking around editor UX, data persistence and fullstack React architecture.

6.title: Robotfriends
6.description: Personal React project for API data rendering, search and filtering.

caseStudy.subtitle: React API Data Project
caseStudy.problem: The project was built to practice rendering remote data, filtering lists and handling basic UI states in React.
caseStudy.myRole: I implemented the UI, fetched user data from an API and built search and filtering interactions around reusable components.
whatIDid: Rendered API data into reusable card components
Added search and filtering for the user list
Handled loading and error states
Practiced component composition and state updates
Deployed the project as a small public demo
caseStudy.outcome: The project strengthened my fundamentals around React components, API data and interactive UI state.

7.title: Trillo
7.description: Static responsive layout project focused on Flexbox, Sass and SVG styling.

caseStudy.subtitle: Responsive Layout Practice
caseStudy.problem: I wanted to practice building a polished static interface with responsive layout techniques and more advanced CSS tooling.
caseStudy.myRole: I implemented the page layout and styling from scratch, focusing on Flexbox structure, Sass organization and responsive behavior.
whatIDid: Built a static hotel booking-style layout
Used Flexbox for the main page structure
Organized styles with Sass
Practiced custom CSS properties, masks and SVG assets
Adjusted the layout across responsive breakpoints
caseStudy.outcome: The project gave me stronger confidence in responsive CSS, layout composition and visual implementation details.

8.title: Simple ToDo list
8.description: Small jQuery ToDo app with task creation, completion states and simple animations.

caseStudy.subtitle: Interactive ToDo App
caseStudy.problem: The goal was to practice DOM manipulation and basic interactive app behavior before moving deeper into component frameworks.
caseStudy.myRole: I built the task interactions, UI states and small CSS effects using jQuery and plain frontend fundamentals.
whatIDid: Added task creation and removal
Implemented checked and unchecked task states
Used jQuery for DOM updates and event handling
Added small CSS animations and hover effects
Practiced structuring a simple interactive UI
caseStudy.outcome: The project helped solidify the JavaScript and DOM basics that later made React state management easier to understand.

9.title: RGB Game
9.description: Small color guessing game for practicing JavaScript events and DOM updates.

caseStudy.subtitle: Color Guessing Game
caseStudy.problem: The game was a compact way to practice user interaction, random state generation and visual feedback in plain JavaScript.
caseStudy.myRole: I implemented the game logic, color generation, difficulty behavior and DOM updates for the UI.
whatIDid: Generated random RGB color options
Handled user guesses and feedback states
Added easy and hard game modes
Updated the interface with plain DOM manipulation
Practiced event handling and small app logic
caseStudy.outcome: The project reinforced core JavaScript concepts through a simple, visual and interactive browser game.
